sections.paragraph:
fut. יָרוּץ, תָּרֻץ (for יָרֹץ), Isa 42:4 Ecc 12:6, compare אֲרוּצֵם Jer 50:44 כתיב (Arab. رَضَّ) cogn. to רָעַע and רָעַץ.

(1) to break, to break in or down. Isa 42:3, קָנֶה רָצוּץ “a crushed reed,” das eingeknickte Rohr (Vulg. quassata). Isa 36:6 2Ki 18:21. It differs from שָׁבַר to break off, see Isa 42:3.-Intrans. to be broken, Ecc. loc. cit.

(2) figuratively, to oppress, to treat violently, often joined with עָשַׁק 1Sa 12:3, 1Sa 12:4 Amo 4:1 Isa 58:6 Deu 28:33.

Niphal נָרֹוץ pass. of Kal No. 1, Ecc 12:6 Eze 29:7.

Piel רִצַּץ-

(1) i.q. Kal No. 1, but more forcible, to break in pieces, Psa 74:14.

(2) i.q. Kal No. 2, Job 20:19 2Ch 16:10.

Poel רֹוצֵץ i.q. Kal and Piel No. 2, Jud. 10:8 . But רֹוצֵץ Nah 2:5, belongs to the root רוּץ to run.

Hiphil, fut. conv. וַתָּרִץ (so as to differ from וַתָּרֶץ to make to run), Jud. 9:53 , to break in pieces.

Hithpolel, to dash one another, to struggle together, Gen 25:22.

Derivatives, רַץ, compare מְרוּצָה No. II.
